title: [Bug]: RUN --mount=from=stage layer caching issue
body:
### Issue Description

A layer with `RUN --mount=from=otherstage` is reused from cache even though `otherstage` has changed.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Assume the following Containerfile

```
FROM alpine as dependencies

RUN mkdir /build && echo v1 > /build/version

FROM alpine

RUN --mount=type=bind,source=/build,target=/build,from=dependencies \
    cp /build/version /version

CMD cat /version
```

Run `podman build -t test .`.

Then change `v1` to `v2` in the Containerfile, run `podman build -t test .` again, and notice the second RUN layer is reused from cache.

### Describe the results you received

The second build reuses the RUN --mount layer from cache. 
Running resulting image outputs `v1`.

### Describe the results you expected

The second RUN --mount layer should be rebuilt after changing the `dependencies` layer. 
Running the resulting image should output `v2`.

### podman info output

comments.body: Hi @sbidoul , Thanks for reporting this is a bug with buildah.
